sections.paragraph:
μαθητεία, ας, ἡ (s. μαθητής) lesson, instruction (Timo [III b.c.] 54 in Poet. Philos.; Dio Chrys. 4, 41; AscIs 3:13; Just., D. 53, 6; Suda) οὗ τὸ κατάστημα μεγάλη μ. whose demeanor is a great lesson ITr 3:2.—DELG s.v. μανθάνω.

Poet. Philos. Poet. Philos. = Poetarum Philosophorum fragmenta—List 5

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

AscIs AscIs = Ascension of Isaiah—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Suda Suda = a lexical work (freq. cited as Suidas in handbooks); X a.d.—List 5

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 609
